free_text: Neutron Total Cross Sections in the kev Region by
The neutron detector is a slab of compacted
10B, closely backed by a 3x3 NaI(Tl) scintillation
cylinders, canned in thin-walled silver cups for
protection. Samples were used either in elemental
form or as oxides or fluorides.
The energy range from 2 to 30 keV has usually
been covered with about five flight path positions,
The approximate total background for maximum flight
paths varies from 30% at 2 keV to 10% at 10 keV.
Correction for the fluoride or oxygen cross section
was made assuming sigma(F)=3.7 barns and sigma(O)=3.8
